# Source Card: Prevention, Screening, Diagnosis, and Treatment of Iron Deficiency and Iron Deficiency Anemia in Infants, Children, and Adolescents: Clinical Report

## Summary
This 2026 AAP Clinical Report (Pediatrics 2026;158(1):e2026077414) is the primary current authority for the pediatric-CDS anemia module's iron-deficiency logic. It establishes the CBC + serum ferritin as the two core diagnostic tests, provides age- and sex-specific Hb/MCV/RDW normal intervals (Table 1), sets serum ferritin ID thresholds of ≤20 ng/mL (young/school-aged children) and ≤30 ng/mL (adolescents and all menstruating individuals), specifies ferritin/CRP and sTfR/log10-ferritin interpretation, frames microcytic differentiation among IDA, thalassemia trait, and anemia of inflammation, and defines severe IDA as Hb <7 g/dL. This card backfills exact-passage locators for anemia KB id AAP2026_IDA; every numeric threshold it backs was located verbatim in the full text.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) CBC (Hb/MCV/RDW) + serum ferritin are the two core ID/IDA tests, performed together — locator: Screening > Laboratory Screening — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:36ae0e035e1e4df6f2e428c4ee0e3560a6bf6dcecc346588440ce1aabe6c5865]"
- (ev_002) Age/sex-specific normal Hb, MCV, RDW intervals — locator: Table 1 (Hb / MCV / RDW rows; 6–24 mo, 2–6 y, 6–12 y, 12–<18 y, Females/Males) — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:64d018a253d96b925cfe2ef202783286556eebca90f1270844a24268a7dcb09b]"
- (ev_003) Ferritin ID thresholds ≤20 / ≤30 ng/mL — locator: Diagnosis > Iron Studies — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:f5ec859298b7cc9a8500a191109c744319357c839d748f8b224163de91af3ba7]"
- (ev_004) Ferritin is an acute-phase reactant; elevated CRP invalidates a normal SF — locator: Diagnosis > Iron Studies — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:42f51243d3a32b8cc9b12b9cf05439aaed9522e95f732b9b288ec632d40e671f]"
- (ev_005) sTfR/log10-ferritin index >2 (ID) vs <1 (anemia of chronic inflammation) — locator: Diagnosis > Iron Studies + Table 4 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:e063ac22b71917f3ced3bfbb91e7acdcb6816641364876c162f037025c7cecf2]"
- (ev_006) Microcytic differentiation: IDA vs thalassemia trait vs anemia of inflammation — locator: Laboratory Diagnosis of IDA > CBC + Table 3 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:669a1f5e1e73898941f26d67260ee553aa319f8b4f789515729a62e60833eb38]"
- (ev_007) Severe IDA = confirmed IDA with Hb <7 g/dL (not a universal transfusion trigger) — locator: Treatment of Severe or Refractory IDA > Severe IDA — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:8a0d40f4304772815beb0ad2b114052661ef877eb6e8ee458fd5735c9d461a7e]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Age bands defined in the report: infancy (birth–<12 mo), toddlers/early childhood (≥12 mo–<5 y), school age (≥5–<11 y), adolescence (≥11–<21 y). Table 1 intervals are stratified by sex and by 6–24 mo / 2–6 y / 6–12 y / 12–<18 y.
- Ferritin thresholds are method- and inflammation-dependent: SF is an acute-phase reactant, so ferritin cutoffs (≤20/≤30 ng/mL) apply only when CRP is not elevated / the child is clinically well; otherwise use sTfR/log10-ferritin index.
- Guideline lifecycle: AAP Clinical Report 2026, published Pediatrics July 2026; replaces the 2010 AAP clinical report. Ferritin thresholds supersede longstanding 10–12 ng/mL norms and diverge from WHO 2020 (<12 ng/mL young children; <15 ng/mL ≥5 y), aligning with ASH (≤20/≤30 ng/mL).

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- Severe IDA (Hb <7 g/dL) with hemodynamic instability, poor intake, heart failure, or uncontrolled blood loss → transfusion + close monitoring (scope-exit to acute care); stable severe IDA may be managed outpatient with close follow-up.
- Lymphadenopathy, hepatosplenomegaly, bruising, petechiae, or jaundice are "not features of IDA" and should prompt evaluation for other etiologies (referral trigger).
- Refractory/persistent/recurrent IDA and non-IDA microcytic causes (thalassemia syndromes, sideroblastic anemia) warrant hematology referral.

## Limitations & conflicts
- Table 1 title states "From Age 9 Months to 18 Years" while the KB describes the fallback intervals as "6 months to <18 years" and the first table band is "6 to 24 mo"; the minor label/age-band mismatch is preserved here rather than reconciled — treat the verbatim Table 1 age bands as authoritative.
- Ferritin-threshold conflict is preserved, not collapsed: WHO 2020 (<12 / <15 ng/mL) vs ASH / AAP 2026 (≤20 / ≤30 ng/mL) are different provenances; the CDS should not average them. (WHO cutoffs are carried by KB id WHO2024_HB / WHO ferritin guidance, not this card.)
- sTfR/log10-ferritin index lacks published norms in healthy children and has limited availability (stated in source); the >2 / <1 cutpoints are the report's, not lab-universal.
- Threshold values are diagnostic-classification proposals for the CDS converter, not executable diagnosis/treatment/transfusion directives; the Hb <7 g/dL severe-IDA category is explicitly not a universal transfusion threshold.
